rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance.
Ritual / practice (as noun) / ceremony — hard ban
No stale markers, no "fixed" retrospectives, no session biography.
Register match.
Rough-cut drafts for articles.
Credit the original when paraphrasing a named technique.
Always "you", never "the student"; teacher framing stays out of body.
Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
Over-hedge detector.
Verbatim-frame-cite in reference artefact.
Cap practitioner attribution at one student-side mention per module.
Punchy framing keeps its punch; carve-out lives downstream.
Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + project-level strategy docs, NOT in student-facing curriculum body.
Watch out for planting jargon too early.
Audit the closer when trimming a body-prose forcing function.
No combative verbs about the agent in body prose.
Aspirational destination, never corrective gap.
Earn every technical term on first use.
Business-audience word ban (Bootstrap default).
Memory vs context — use deliberately.
Golden path only in the body.
Q1/Q2/Q3 retro interview is banned at every module.
Hard-grep phrase list — room-share commands and contemplative-pause theatre.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 45: "Comparing your first read to theirs is the thing you name aloud." — 'aloud' approaches the room-share register (cf. banned 'say it aloud', 'read aloud'); in Key Concepts this reads as a mandated vocalization cue
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Recast to describe the comparison act without aloud: 'Comparing your first read to theirs is where the gap becomes legible.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No narrating the student's past or present state.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 8: "Two modules of agent rhythm sit behind you." — narrates student's completed-module history as a calibration frame; closer pat-on-head shape
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Flip to arc/artifact-side state: 'Two modules of agent rhythm are in.' or cut — the feature-size rule carries without the history-drumbeat.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No em-dashes. Simple sentences.
Section headers use command verbs when the section is about doing something.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 64: "## Ready to clear" — non-canonical section, body has student signaling and clearing the session, but header is a readiness-state phrase not a command verb
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Rename to a command verb: '## Clear the session' or '## Signal the wrap-up'.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: LLM-vs-agent-vs-Claude vocabulary discipline.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 67: "Claude converges" and 75: "If Claude names something missed" — both describe autonomous multi-step scanning/summarizing actions in session; rule 21 routes autonomous acting to 'the agent', product/addressee to 'Claude'
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Swap to 'the agent converges' and 'if the agent names something missed' — the actions are agentic (scrollback scan, summary); Claude as product name is for invocation / UI surface references.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No time-of-day anchors in body prose.
Reflection beats stay invisible in the flow.
Cut NVA: true but not moving the student forward.
